items.q: What is the denary value of binary 10110?
items.why: Students forget to apply place values. Each position is a power of 2 from right (1, 2, 4, 8, 16...). Reading binary as denary is the most common error.

items.q: What does it mean that RAM is volatile?
items.correct: Its contents are lost when power is removed.
items.misconception: It can explode or be damaged easily.
items.why: Students take 'volatile' from chemistry literally. In computing, volatile means contents don't persist without power - that's why RAM is used for working memory, not storage.

items.q: Why must a recursive function have a base case?
items.correct: Without it, the function calls itself indefinitely, causing a stack overflow.
items.random_wrong: To make the function run faster.
items.why: Students treat the base case as optional ceremony. It's the TERMINATION CONDITION that stops recursion - each call adds a stack frame, and without termination the stack will overflow.

items.q: Which describes a network in a single building?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: LAN. “WAN” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many layers does the OSI model have?
items.why: Exact answer: 7 layers. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
